mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals an impending monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite PAYEMS growth, or t-test p > 0.05 for growth periods vs baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts (HOUST) reflect strong demand for residential construction, which correlates with broader economic optimism and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite HOUST growth, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in residential sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals a synchronized housing market expansion that drives equity demand through improved consumer confidence and corporate sector activity.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase > 5% in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite RSAFS/HOUST growth, or interaction effect not significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL > 240) combined with weak industrial production (INDPRO < 105) indicates stagflation risks that erode corporate margins and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite CPIAUCSL/INDPRO signals, or interaction effect not significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1 > 2%) reflects sustained economic expansion that supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of GDP growth periods, or t-test p > 0.05 for growth periods vs baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE < 4.5%) indicates tightening labor markets that drive wage inflation and corporate cost pressures, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite UNRATE declines, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%) and low unemployment (UNRATE < 5%) signal an accommodative monetary policy environment that boosts equity risk appetite and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS/UNRATE signals, or interaction effect not significant (p > 0.05).
